stories.headline: Iran suspends mediated US talks and threatens to fully close Hormuz, blaming Israel's Lebanon offensive; Trump says talks continue
stories.summary: Iran on June 1 suspended its indirect, mediator-run negotiations with Washington — days after the sides neared a 60-day memorandum to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and launch nuclear-track talks — citing Israel's deepening Lebanon operation as a ceasefire violation 'on all fronts.' Tehran vowed to move toward fully closing Hormuz, while President Trump countered that talks are continuing 'at a rapid pace' and that Iran never informed Washington of any halt.

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran breaks off war-ending diplomacy and threatens a global shipping chokepoint, sending oil sharply higher and US allies on edge.
perspectives.summary: Center wires report Iran broke off the mediator-run talks aimed at ending the conflict and reopening Hormuz after Israel's escalation in Lebanon, with Tehran threatening to 'completely' block the strait. Coverage notes oil jumped more than 6% and that CENTCOM reportedly intercepted missiles headed toward Kuwait, underscoring how quickly the diplomatic track unraveled.

sources.title: Iran says it is breaking off talks to end war after U.S. and Israeli strikes
Iran halts talks with U.S. over Israeli actions in Lebanon, Gaza

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump downplays the rupture, insisting negotiations proceed and dismissing Iranian state-media claims as posturing.
perspectives.summary: Trump posted that 'talks are continuing, at a rapid pace, with the Islamic Republic of Iran,' said Tehran 'haven't informed us' of any suspension, and told CNBC he 'couldn't care less' if they collapse. The administration-aligned read casts Iran's walkout as leverage-seeking while a deal remains, in Trump's words, 'largely negotiated.'

sources.title: Iran stops negotiations with U.S., vows to 'completely' block Strait of Hormuz: State media

perspectives.keyFraming: Tehran's framing: a ceasefire violated 'on one front is a violation on all fronts,' so Washington and Israel own the consequences.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera relays the Iranian position that Israeli operations in Lebanon and Gaza breached a ceasefire understood to cover 'all fronts,' threatening the US-mediated process. FM Araghchi warned the US and Israel would be 'responsible for the consequences,' while IRGC figures floated disrupting traffic through Hormuz and Bab al-Mandab.

sources.title: Iran warns Israeli attacks in Lebanon and Gaza threaten US ceasefire talks

stories.headline: Far-right populist de la Espriella tops Colombia first round; June 21 runoff vs leftist Cepeda will reset US ties
stories.summary: With ~99.99% counted, Trump-aligned hard-right outsider Abelardo de la Espriella took roughly 43.7% (10M votes) to leftist Iván Cepeda's 40.9% in Colombia's first-round May 31 vote, sending the contest to a June 21 runoff that pits a populist against an ally of outgoing President Petro. De la Espriella called on Washington to 'watch the runoff closely'; turnout was about 56% (23M voters).

perspectives.keyFraming: De la Espriella is the runoff favorite, strong among the South Florida diaspora, on a hardline security mandate.
perspectives.summary: WLRN frames de la Espriella's first-round lead as a backlash against the left, highlighting his support among Colombian voters in South Florida and his 'peace imposed by force of arms' security stance heading into the runoff against Cepeda.

sources.title: Right-winger De la Espriella is Colombia's presidential runoff favorite — especially in South Florida

stories.headline: Tribal fighting erupts in South Darfur with RSF vehicles as Sudan's war deepens famine risk
stories.summary: Fighting between the Salamat and Beni Halba tribes reignited around Kubum in South Darfur in late May, with both sides deploying RSF vehicles; villages were burned and thousands displaced as the violence spread toward Central Darfur. The clashes compound a humanitarian collapse in which roughly 19.5 million Sudanese face acute food insecurity and more than a dozen areas across Darfur and Kordofan are at risk of famine.

perspectives.keyFraming: RSF-linked tribal violence deepens Sudan's humanitarian collapse — a periphery US wires rarely cover.
perspectives.summary: Sudan Tribune reports the clashes burned villages and forced thousands to flee, with both tribes using RSF vehicles, underscoring how Sudan's national war is fragmenting into local conflicts. The framing centers the civilian toll and the spread of fighting into Central Darfur rather than great-power dynamics.

sources.title: Villages burned and civilians flee as tribal clashes erupt in South Darfur using RSF vehicles

perspectives.keyFraming: A war of attrition intensifies across Darfur and Kordofan as famine risk escalates.
perspectives.summary: Security Council Report's June forecast situates the fighting within Sudan's broader drone-and-artillery war and a widening hunger crisis, noting roughly 19.5 million people facing acute food insecurity and multiple areas at risk of famine as humanitarian access remains obstructed.

sources.title: Sudan: June 2026 Monthly Forecast

stories.headline: Rubio says Netanyahu's goal of seizing 70% of Gaza contradicts Trump's plan, exposing a rare US-Israel rift
stories.summary: At a June 2 congressional hearing, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Prime Minister Netanyahu's stated goal of taking over 70% of Gaza is not part of President Trump's 20-point framework, saying 'we have a plan — it doesn't call for that.' The rare public daylight between Washington and Jerusalem followed Netanyahu's late-May directive to the IDF to expand control of the Strip, which critics say threatens the ceasefire framework.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israeli coverage flags rare daylight between Washington and Jerusalem over the war's endgame in Gaza.
perspectives.summary: The Times of Israel reports Secretary of State Marco Rubio told a congressional hearing that Netanyahu's stated goal of seizing 70% of Gaza is not part of Trump's plan — 'we have a plan, it doesn't call for that' — adding the aim is a Gaza governed by a non-Hamas entity. The remark followed Netanyahu's late-May directive to expand the military's control of the Strip.

sources.title: Rubio says Netanyahu's goal to seize 70% of Gaza contradicts Trump's plan for Strip

perspectives.keyFraming: US coverage situates the rift within fragile Israel-Lebanon-Iran diplomacy playing out in Washington.
perspectives.summary: CNN's live coverage notes the US-Israel friction over Gaza's endgame as Israeli and Lebanese envoys held US-brokered talks in Washington and the Iran ceasefire file remained unresolved, framing Rubio's comments as a public signal that Netanyahu's expanded-control plan sits outside Trump's 20-point framework.

sources.title: Israel and Lebanon hold talks in US after Hezbollah and Israel agree to halt hostilities

stories.headline: Rubio tells Senate that US-Iran nuclear and Hormuz talks are at an impasse as Tehran halts messaging
stories.summary: Testifying to the Senate on June 2 — his first appearance since the Iran war began — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US is still in contact with Iran and there is 'the prospect' Tehran could negotiate aspects of its nuclear program, but set conditions: Iran must declare the Strait of Hormuz open, end tolls, help clear mines and not fire on commercial ships. Iran's Fars agency said the sides stopped exchanging messages days earlier; PBS called the talks an impasse.

perspectives.keyFraming: Reports Rubio holding open the prospect of nuclear talks while attaching hard conditions on the Strait of Hormuz.
perspectives.summary: CNBC and PBS NewsHour report Rubio told senators the administration remains in contact with Iran and sees a prospect of negotiating its nuclear program, but only if Tehran declares Hormuz open, stops charging tolls, helps clear mines and pledges not to fire on commercial vessels — with both outlets characterizing the current state of talks as an impasse.

sources.title: Rubio says U.S. is in talks with Iran over nuclear program as senators press for war endgame
U.S.-Iran talks at impasse over nuclear program and Strait of Hormuz

perspectives.keyFraming: Centers Iran's account that it suspended messaging because of Israel's Lebanon assault, not nuclear brinkmanship.
perspectives.summary: Democracy Now frames the breakdown from Tehran's vantage point, reporting Iran suspended its exchange of messages with Washington over Israel's expanding operations in Lebanon and Gaza, and tying the standoff to the wider regional escalation rather than to US demands over the Strait of Hormuz alone.

sources.title: Iran Suspends U.S. Talks as Israel Kills More in Lebanon & Expands Occupation

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon open a new round of direct talks in Washington on enforcing their fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: Israeli and Lebanese envoys opened a new round of direct talks at the US State Department on June 2, scheduled over two days, after President Trump said he had secured de-escalation commitments from both Jerusalem and Hezbollah. The US side, led by Deputy National Security Adviser Mike Needham and Ambassador Mike Huckabee, focused the agenda on ceasefire enforcement and a 'move-for-move' mechanism — even as fighting intensified in south Lebanon.

perspectives.keyFraming: Process-focused coverage of a rare direct round between two states still technically at war since 1948.
perspectives.summary: By June 3 the sides had reached a fourth round of direct talks at the State Department, with US spokesman Tommy Pigott saying 'progress continues on the political and security tracks' toward a comprehensive agreement — even as Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon killed five and damaged Tebnine Governmental Hospital. The US side is led by Deputy NSA Mike Needham, chief of staff Daniel Holler and Ambassador Mike Huckabee, with the agenda centered on ceasefire enforcement and a step-by-step de-escalation mechanism.

sources.title: US claims Lebanon talks making progress despite deadly Israeli strikes
New round of Israel-Lebanon talks begins in Washington

perspectives.keyFraming: Frames the Washington roadmap against continued Israeli strikes, questioning whether de-escalation can hold.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera reports a US official outlined a roadmap for de-escalation in Lebanon ahead of the Washington talks, situating the diplomacy within ongoing Israeli operations in the south and skepticism among Lebanese and regional observers about whether commitments to halt fighting will be honored on the ground.

sources.title: Washington proposes roadmap for de-escalation in Lebanon, US official says

stories.headline: Kuwait expels two Iranian diplomats and summons envoy after deadly airport strike; India condemns attack
stories.summary: After an Iranian strike killed one person — an Indian national — and injured more than 60 at Kuwait International Airport, Kuwait summoned Iran's chargé d'affaires, delivered a formal protest, declared two Iranian diplomats persona non grata with 24 hours to leave, and reduced the embassy's staff. India condemned the attack on civilian infrastructure, signaling sharp Gulf diplomatic blowback against Tehran beyond the battlefield.

perspectives.keyFraming: A Gulf non-combatant retaliates diplomatically; the death of an Indian worker draws New Delhi's condemnation and regional alarm.
perspectives.summary: Kuwait's deputy foreign minister summoned Iran's chargé d'affaires, expelled two diplomats and cut embassy staffing after the airport strike that killed an Indian national and wounded dozens. India condemned the attack on civilian infrastructure. Coverage emphasized the human toll on Gulf civilians and migrant workers caught in the US-Iran crossfire.

sources.title: Kuwait demands two Iran embassy staff leave after deadly attack

perspectives.keyFraming: Chinese state media reports the expulsions factually, framing the escalation as a regional spillover testing the ceasefire.
perspectives.summary: CGTN reported Kuwait summoning the Iranian envoy and expelling two diplomats amid the Iranian attacks, presenting the diplomatic rupture within a broader narrative of a fragile US-Iran ceasefire under strain rather than assigning blame.

sources.title: Kuwait summons Iranian envoy, expels 2 diplomats amid Iranian attacks

stories.headline: Russia opens St. Petersburg economic forum under 'Pragmatic Dialogue' theme, eyeing Western re-engagement
stories.summary: The St. Petersburg International Economic Forum opened June 3, running to June 6, under the theme 'Pragmatic Dialogue: the Path to a Stable Future,' with around 20,000 guests from more than 130 countries. The Kremlin used it to project economic outreach and signal openness to Western business returning, even as Ukrainian drones struck the city hours before delegates arrived. Putin's plenary address is scheduled for June 5.

perspectives.keyFraming: SPIEF showcases Russia's resilience and global outreach; state media floats a Western business 'return' despite the war.
perspectives.summary: TASS and Xinhua framed the forum as proof of Russia's economic openness, with Putin set to deliver a major plenary speech on June 5 and outreach to more than 100 countries under a pragmatism theme, and Saudi Arabia as guest country. State coverage cast the gathering as a counter-narrative to Western isolation and raised the question of whether Western firms are quietly returning.

sources.title: Putin to deliver grand speech at SPIEF 2026 — Kremlin aide
Russia's flagship economic forum opens in St. Petersburg

perspectives.keyFraming: Putin's prestige forum opens awkwardly under Ukrainian fire; outreach claims meet the reality of a grinding war.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera described the forum as Putin's economic outreach to the Global South and 'friendly' nations amid Western estrangement, noting it opened just hours after Ukrainian drones struck St. Petersburg energy facilities — underscoring the gap between the Kremlin's stability messaging and the ongoing conflict.

sources.title: What is the St Petersburg forum, Putin's economic outreach to the world?
